title: The Great Potato Play Strikes Out
story: Batter up for a wacky baseball tale! On August 31, 1987, minor league catcher Dave Bresnahan cooked up a crazy scheme. During a game, he secretly swapped his baseball for a potato carved to look like one. Bresnahan hoped to trick a runner on third base, but his plan went splat. The umpire wasn't fooled and called the runner safe. Talk about a half-baked idea! The team found Bresnahan's stunt un-apeeling, fining him and releasing him from the roster.

follow_up_question: Why do you think the umpire called the runner safe?

title: La Nina's Triple Trouble
story: Unusual weather patterns made waves in 2022. The UN weather agency announced a rare 'triple dip' La Nina event would last until year's end. This cool ocean current affects global weather, causing droughts in some areas. Experts warned it could worsen dry conditions in the Horn of Africa and South America. It was the first triple La Nina this century on August 31.
activity_question: What type of weather event did the UN agency predict?
follow_up_question: How might a La Nina event affect the weather where you live?
